payload.answer: You have two conflicting family-role memories for “Sara”: one says she is your daughter, the other says she is your niece. These roles generally cannot both be true for the same person relative to you at the same time, so the system should treat them as a contradiction and keep ambiguity until you clarify which statement is correct (or whether “Sara” refers to different people).
payload.conflict_classification: Direct relationship contradiction for the same named entity: Sara is my daughter vs Sara is my niece (inconsistent family roles).
payload.decision: Keep both statements as potentially different relationships unless additional context disambiguates whether “Sara” refers to the same person and what role is intended (daughter vs niece). Do not mark either as superseding the other.

entity_resolution.reason: Both statements use the same name (“Sara”) but provide mutually incompatible family roles (daughter vs niece). Without additional context, it is unclear whether this is a true contradiction about one person or a mismatch about which Sara is being referenced.

details: Detected contradiction between existing and input memory statements about Sara’s relationship to you.
No durable overwrite performed; both statements retained as non-superseding hypotheses pending clarification.

repair_options: Confirm whether “Sara” in both statements is the same person; if not, rename one entry (e.g., include last name or distinguishing detail).
Indicate which relationship is correct (daughter vs niece).
Add context that could resolve edge cases (e.g., adopted/foster status, step-relationships, or whether “niece” is being used loosely).
If you meant different roles (e.g., daughter-in-law or stepdaughter), specify the exact relationship term.
